cohort_gender: All female (obstetric/pregnant or recently postpartum women)
notes: Mean lactate at diagnosis was 2.5 mmol/L (SD 2.1) in survivors vs 6.2 mmol/L (SD 5.2) in those who died (P=.05)

cohort_gender: All female (obstetric/pregnant or recently postpartum women)

cohort_gender: All female (obstetric/pregnant or recently postpartum women)

effect_size: 59.1% of patients who died had delayed antibiotic initiation vs 45.6% overall received antibiotics within 1 hour
notes: 48% of patients were already receiving antibiotics before sepsis diagnosis; no formal statistical test result reported for this comparison

population: Sepsis patients during hospitalization
cohort_age: Mean age 27.1 years (SD=6.8) in non-survivors vs. 23.6 years (SD=6.6) in survivors
cohort_gender: All female (obstetric/pregnant or recently postpartum women)
performance: Only factor remaining associated with death after logistic regression
notes: Lactate level and SOFA score were associated with death in bivariate analysis but did not remain significant after logistic regression, likely due to collinearity with vasopressor use.

population: Patients with sepsis admitted to hospital
cohort_age: Mean age 27.1 years (SD=6.8) in non-survivors vs. 23.6 years (SD=6.6) in survivors
cohort_gender: All female (obstetric/pregnant or recently postpartum women)
performance: Statistically significant association with death in multivariate analysis
notes: Septic shock at admission was associated with death in bivariate analysis but not in multivariate analysis. 36.4% of patients who died had septic shock at admission; 90.9% of patients who died received vasopressor drugs. Presence of comorbidities (analyzed together) was not associated with death.

population: Pregnancy-associated severe sepsis patients admitted to ICU
cohort_age: Mean age 27.1 years (SD=6.8) in non-survivors vs. 23.6 years (SD=6.6) in survivors
cohort_gender: All female (obstetric/pregnant or recently postpartum women)
notes: Lactate >2 mmol/L in 81.8% of patients who died. Single-center study.

population: Patients admitted to obstetric ICU with sepsis
cohort_age: Mean age 27.1 years (SD=6.8) in non-survivors vs. 23.6 years (SD=6.6) in survivors
cohort_gender: All female (obstetric/pregnant or recently postpartum women)
effect_size: Median SOFA 2 (IQR 0–5) in survivors vs. 6 (IQR 4–9.5) in non-survivors; SOFA >2 in 85% of deaths
performance: Statistically significant association with death (p-value not reported)
notes: SOFA score was not used for diagnosis during the data collection period but was included for organ/system failure assessment. Study is retrospective, single-center, obstetric ICU.

population: Obstetric patients with sepsis in an ICU in Brazil
cohort_age: Mean age 27.1 years (SD=6.8) in non-survivors vs. 23.6 years (SD=6.6) in survivors
cohort_gender: All female (obstetric/pregnant or recently postpartum women)
notes: Vasopressor use was identified as the main factor associated with death in obstetric patients with sepsis.
